growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Defined anoxic mineral medium (hydrogenotrophic DNRA), chemostat

up.method: reported steady-state reduction rate (2 mM fully reduced to NH4+)

sec.method: inferred = NO3- reduction rate (2 mM NO3- completely reduced to NH4+)

growth_records.cite: Heo et al. Hydrogen-dependent dissimilatory nitrate reduction to ammonium enables growth of Campylobacterota isolates. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf092. [PMC12286921]
growth_records.snippet: Only after H2 and CO2 concentrations in the gas stream were increased to 1% and 5%, respectively, the reactor completely reduced 2 mM NO3- to NH4+ at a rate of 15.65 ± 0.24 µmol h-1. During this steady-state operation, acetate was consumed
growth_records.notes: Chemostat dilution D=0.025 h-1 per Methods (gas ratios/flow adjusted for this strain but medium dilution rate not restated as changed); mu=D=0.025 h-1, doubling=ln2/0.025=27.7 h. NH4+ production rate taken equal to NO3- reduction rate 15.65 µmol/h by DNRA stoichiometry (complete NO3->NH4). Removal of H2 caused washout (H2-dependent). Reactor-level rates; no biomass conc reported.

growth_records.medium: Defined anoxic mineral medium (hydrogenotrophic DNRA), batch

up.method: whole-cell Michaelis-Menten; Vmax(app)=3.01 µmol/min/mg biomass, Km(app)=15.0 µM (95% CI 11.5-19.5)
1910 ± 20 µmol H2 oxidized = 3820 µmol e- (exceeded theoretical demand 1330 ± 10 µmol e-; ~30% electrons to assimilation)
2 mM (200 µmol) NO3- reduced to NH4+

sec.method: NH4+ produced from NO3- reduction; also produced NH4+ on H2 without acetate (P<.05)

growth_records.cite: Heo et al. Hydrogen-dependent dissimilatory nitrate reduction to ammonium enables growth of Campylobacterota isolates. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf092. [PMC12286921]
growth_records.snippet: Sulfurospirillum sp. hDNRA2 achieved significantly higher biomass (OD600 value of 0.064 ± 0.001...). The electron yield from H2 oxidation (1910 ± 20 µmol e-) greatly exceeded the theoretical electron demand (1330 ± 10 µmol e-)... Vmax(app)
growth_records.notes: Batch H2+acetate+NO3-. No mu/doubling reported. H2 specific uptake Vmax(app)=3.01 µmol/min/mg x 60 = 180.6 mmol/gDW/h. Totals are per-bottle over batch, not rates. Final OD600 0.064±0.001. H2 sole e- donor (acetate not coupled to NO3- reduction in this strain); ~30% of H2-derived electrons allocated to assimilation.
